acceptance_criteria.text: The left sidebar is reduced to the app title, the existing new-project-button, and the project/session navigation area; extra footer/status chrome outside that navigation area is removed.

proof.summary: The sidebar only renders the title/new-project header and the project/session nav region.

acceptance_criteria.text: Project rows remain usable for expand/collapse and session creation, while session rows stay selectable via session-row with simplified status-focused summaries.

proof.summary: Project rows keep expand/collapse and new-session-button, and session rows are simplified to name plus status.
Playwright usage flows passed with the simplified project/session list.

acceptance_criteria.text: The active session header keeps active-session-label, the Start/Stop/New Panel controls, one project chip, and the current session/agent status chips; runtime/workspace text, agent URL text, sandbox chip, and docker availability chip are removed.

proof.summary: The active session header renders only the simplified label/chip/control set with no retired metadata subtree.
Focused Playwright verification confirmed the removed header metadata selectors are absent.

acceptance_criteria.text: With an active session open, the workspace layout fills available height without page-level vertical scrolling at normal desktop sizes; vertical scrolling is contained inside the sidebar or panel content areas, and horizontal scrolling is limited to the session panel rail.

proof.summary: App shell styles and workspace containers constrain layout height and keep outer overflow hidden.
Fresh verification measured no page-level vertical overflow and confirmed the panel rail is the horizontal scroll container.

acceptance_criteria.text: The Files panel header no longer repeats the workspace path, and opening a file from the tree opens or focuses the matching editor panel, then scrolls the rail so that editor into view directly without duplicates.

proof.summary: The Files panel header drops the redundant path, and openFile deduplicates editors and scrolls the target editor into view.
File-open and editor-focus flows passed, including one-editor-per-file behavior.

acceptance_criteria.text: The Editor panel keeps the file title, relative file path, and existing save/close controls in the main header, removes the duplicate path/status strip, keeps editor scrolling inside the editor body, and preserves editor-active-path on the visible path element.

proof.summary: The visible editor path moved into the main header and keeps the editor-active-path selector.
The editor body is the only scrollable editor content region, with no duplicate footer strip.

acceptance_criteria.text: The Terminal panel fills the full panel body height, uses a solid theme-aware background, and still starts, resizes, accepts input, and stops against the current session through the existing terminal integration.

proof.summary: The terminal uses a solid theme-aware palette and preserves start/write/resize/stop integration.
Fresh verification confirmed full-height mounting, solid background, typed input echo, and clean stop/restart behavior.

acceptance_criteria.text: The Browser panel is simplified so navigation is driven from the top URL row, Enter submits navigation, the existing web-open-button appears only when the normalized draft URL differs from the loaded URL, redundant subtitle/help/status rows are removed, and the empty state or iframe fills the remaining body area.

proof.summary: Browser navigation is driven from the top URL row with conditional submit affordance and no extra status/help rows.
Browser navigation and iframe loading passed in the usage regression suite.

acceptance_criteria.text: Open panels can be resized narrower or wider from the panel rail without breaking focus, drag-reorder, close, file-open, or browser/terminal flows during the current app session.

proof.summary: Per-session width state, resize handles, and drag/drop ordering are implemented in the panel rail.
The dedicated Playwright regression passed for resize plus horizontal-scroll drag reorder.
